title: T-046 — ref-tables.enum.ts source-of-truth lock (Reference table names registry)
prompt:
# T-046 — ref-tables.enum.ts source-of-truth lock (Reference table names registry)

PRD: `00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §6 (Reference.DeptColumns / FieldTypes / Formulas), §7 (Reference.Rules), §9 (Reference.Departments / dept_overrides), §9.1 (Reference.ManufacturingOperations)
Guide: `_meta/plans/atomic-task-decomposition-guide.md` §5 (architect owner lock — ref-tables.enum.ts blocks downstream)
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`

## Goal
Lock the canonical Reference table-name registry as a TS const enum so every downstream module imports the same string keys (no typo drift). Decomposition guide §5 lists ref-tables.enum.ts as one of 4 architect-owner-locks that gate parallel dispatch.

## Implementation contract
(1) Create lib/reference/ref-tables.enum.ts exporting `export const RefTables = { DeptColumns: 'Reference.DeptColumns', FieldTypes: 'Reference.FieldTypes', Formulas: 'Reference.Formulas', Rules: 'Reference.Rules', Departments: 'Reference.Departments', DeptOverrides: 'Reference.DeptOverrides', ManufacturingOperations: 'Reference.ManufacturingOperations', CodePrefixes: 'Reference.CodePrefixes' } as const;` plus `export type RefTableName = (typeof RefTables)[keyof typeof RefTables];`. (2) Add a barrel re-export in lib/reference/index.ts. (3) Vitest asserting (a) every value is unique, (b) every value matches the regex /^Reference\\.[A-Z][A-Za-z]+$/, (c) the 8 PRD-named tables all appear (DeptColumns, FieldTypes, Formulas, Rules, Departments, DeptOverrides, ManufacturingOperations, CodePrefixes). (4) Add an ESLint rule no-restricted-syntax forbidding string literals matching /^['\"]Reference\\./ outside lib/reference/** to prevent drift.

## Files (scope)
- lib/reference/ref-tables.enum.ts [create]
- lib/reference/index.ts [create]
- lib/reference/__tests__/ref-tables.test.ts [create]
- .eslintrc.cjs [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given lib/reference/ref-tables.enum.ts is imported, when typeof RefTables.DeptColumns is checked, then it equals the literal 'Reference.DeptColumns' and the union RefTableName has exactly 8 members
2. Given the registry is loaded, when the test iterates values, then every entry matches /^Reference\.[A-Z][A-Za-z]+$/ and there are no duplicates
3. Given a developer hardcodes the literal 'Reference.Foo' anywhere outside lib/reference/**, when 'pnpm lint' runs, then ESLint reports no-restricted-syntax and exits non-zero (drift gate)
4. Given the 8 PRD references (§6 DeptColumns/FieldTypes/Formulas, §7 Rules, §9 Departments/DeptOverrides, §9.1 ManufacturingOperations, §7.x CodePrefixes), when the test enumerates RefTables keys, then all 8 are present

## Test strategy
- RED: write lib/reference/__tests__/ref-tables.test.ts covering all four ACs before ref-tables.enum.ts exists
- Run 'pnpm test && pnpm lint'

## Risk red lines
- Do not add table names that are not PRD-grounded — every entry must trace to a §6/§7/§9/§9.1 reference
- Do not use a TS enum (string-enum runtime overhead + no const-folding) — must be 'as const' object per repo convention used in events.enum.ts (T-003)
- Do not skip the ESLint rule — drift is the failure mode the lock is designed to prevent (see decomposition guide §5)

## Dependencies (must be complete first)
- T-001

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED test before implementation) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Source-of-truth registry for Reference.* table names + ESLint guard preventing string-literal drift outside lib/reference/**.

No seed data — each Reference table seeds in its own task
